title: Lesson 42
paragraph: This lesson focuses on practical B1 vocabulary for everyday communication.

words.meaning: to put something together with something else
words.example: Add your name at the top of the page.

words.meaning: to look at something to make sure it is correct
words.example: Check your answers before you leave.

words.japaneseMeaning: 掃除する、きれいにする
words.example: We clean the classroom after lunch.
My brother can cook curry very well.

words.meaning: to find the number of things
words.example: Count the chairs before the meeting.

words.meaning: to produce tears because of sadness, pain, or strong feeling
words.example: The child began to cry after falling down.

words.meaning: to move your body to music
words.example: They dance at the festival every year.

words.meaning: to put clothes on yourself or someone else
words.example: Please dress warmly because it is cold.
Can you fix this broken chair?

words.meaning: to dislike someone or something very much
words.example: I hate waiting in long lines.
